title: Oedipus King of Thebes: Translated into English Rhyming Verse with Explanatory Notes
author: Sophocles
summary: Oedipus, king of Thebes, confronts a devastating pestilence afflicting his city. The play opens with suppliants and a priest appealing for relief. An oracle links the plague to an unavenged crime; Oedipus vows to find the murderer of former king Laius. As inquiries proceed, figures including the blind seer Tiresias, Creon, the queen Jocasta, messengers, a shepherd, and a stranger from Corinth bring conflicting testimony. Ancient rites, oracles, and the Sphinx’s earlier riddle frame the catastrophe. The investigation unravels family secrets, guilt, and pollution, revealing the tragic collision of human action and inscrutable divine will and overwhelming consequences for all.

characters.name: OEDIPUS
characters.lifespan: Elected King of Thebes and married Jocasta; leads inquiry into Laius's death; further fate is revealed later in the play beyond the provided excerpt.

characters.name: JOCASTA
characters.lifespan: Queen of Thebes and widow of Laius who later marries Oedipus; her past actions and their consequences are central to the tragedy; precise fate not detailed in the provided excerpt.

characters.name: CREON
characters.lifespan: Prince of Thebes and brother to Jocasta; acts as intermediary and is involved in the investigation; later developments not fully covered in the excerpt.

characters.name: TIRESIAS
characters.personality: blind seer, enigmatic, ambivalent between wisdom and malignity
characters.lifespan: A professional prophet who confronts Oedipus with uncomfortable truths; his prophecies drive the inquiry though his exact later fate is not given in the excerpt.

characters.name: PRIEST OF ZEUS
characters.personality: religious, supplicating, authoritative voice for the afflicted city
characters.lifespan: Leads the crowd of suppliants in appealing to Oedipus for relief at the play's opening; role limited to the plea and ritual context in the excerpt.

characters.name: A STRANGER (from Corinth)
characters.personality: mysterious, bearer of news and testimony
characters.lifespan: Described as a stranger from Corinth who provides testimony relevant to Oedipus's background; further fate not specified in the excerpt.

characters.name: A SHEPHERD (of King Laius)
characters.personality: reticent, witness to past events, burdened by memory
characters.lifespan: A land-attending shepherd associated with Laius's household who holds important information about Laius's child; his testimony features in the investigation.

characters.name: A MESSENGER (from the Palace)
characters.lifespan: Conveys information to the court; acts as a classical dramatic messenger whose reports advance the plot within the play.

characters.name: CHORUS of the Elders of Thebes
characters.personality: collective voice, conservative, ritualistic, responsive to omens
characters.lifespan: Group of Theban elders who comment on events, perform choral odes, and reflect communal fears; present throughout the drama as moral and religious frame.

characters.name: CROWD OF SUPPLIANTS
characters.lifespan: Men, women, and children who appear at the play's opening seeking deliverance from plague; serve to articulate the city's suffering.

characters.name: LAIUS
characters.personality: referred to as former king; background figure
characters.lifespan: Former King of Thebes before Oedipus; according to the Argument he was slain mysteriously on the road prior to Oedipus's arrival.

characters.name: CADMUS
characters.lifespan: Founder of Thebes, son of Agenor of Sidon; referenced as part of Theban ancestral myth; does not appear in the play.

characters.name: POLYBUS
characters.personality: parental figure in Oedipus's supposed upbringing
characters.lifespan: King of Corinth mentioned as the supposed father of Oedipus; in the excerpt he is referenced but his detailed fate is not specified here.

characters.name: MEROPE
characters.personality: maternal figure in Oedipus's supposed upbringing
characters.lifespan: Queen of Corinth mentioned as the supposed mother of Oedipus; referenced but her fate is not detailed in the provided text.

characters.name: APOLLO
characters.personality: oracular, vengeful, inscrutable divine force in the play
characters.lifespan: God presiding over the oracle of Delphi whose decrees and hostility are central to the religious frame; a divine agent rather than mortal.

characters.name: SPHINX
characters.lifespan: Monstrous creature that beset Thebes with riddles and death until Oedipus solved her riddle and she flung herself from her rock as recounted in the Argument.

unusual_words.definition: the dramatic focus on character rather than plot (compound term)

unusual_words.definition: impulsiveness; rash action
unusual_words.context: Applied in the Preface as a key trait of Oedipus shaping his judgments.

unusual_words.definition: above all; especially

unusual_words.definition: proper name (the translator's former master)

unusual_words.definition: royal; used as a title ('Regius Professor')

unusual_words.definition: a puzzling question or statement posed for solution

unusual_words.definition: a serious dramatic genre dealing with human suffering

unusual_words.definition: shockingly hideous or frightful; unnatural

unusual_words.definition: ancient Greek city (proper noun)
unusual_words.context: Primary setting of the play, repeatedly named in dramatis personae and Argument.

unusual_words.definition: tending to produce harm or suffering; malevolent
unusual_words.context: Used in the Preface to characterize the gods' apparent hostility in the play.

unusual_words.definition: not doubting; accepting without question
unusual_words.context: Used in the Preface in the phrase 'humble and unquestioning piety' discussed by commentators.

unusual_words.definition: the state of gaining intellectual or spiritual understanding
unusual_words.context: Used in the Preface noting absence of later enlightenment from the play's primaeval atmosphere.

unusual_words.definition: traditional beliefs, stories, and customs of a people
unusual_words.context: Invoked in the Preface as a possible root of the Oedipus story in popular legend.

unusual_words.definition: method or skill in artistic creation
unusual_words.context: Frequently discussed in the Preface as the translator analyzes Sophocles's dramatic method.

unusual_words.definition: serving to explain (as in 'Explanatory Notes')
unusual_words.context: Appears in the book title indicating the translator's added notes.

unusual_words.definition: rendered from one language into another
